Synthetic Pressure Equipment Standard (SPES-1)
A fictional standard mirroring the ASME BPVC Section VIII Div. 1 structure for use in anvil.
Part A â€” General Requirements
A-23 Maximum Allowable Stress Values
(a) The maximum allowable stress values for materials used in the construction of pressure
vessels shall be taken from Part M of this Standard. Specifically, allowable stress values for
carbon and low-alloy steels shall be obtained from Table M-1 (see Part M).
(b) Allowable stress values are tabulated at discrete design temperatures. When a design tem-
peraturefallsbetweentwotabulatedvalues,linearinterpolationshallbeused,inaccordance
with A-23(c).
(c) Noextrapolationbeyondthetabulatedtemperaturerangeispermitted. Ifthedesigntemper-
ature exceeds the maximum tabulated temperature for a material, that material shall not be
used.
A-25 Corrosion
(a) Acorrosion allowance(CA)shallbeaddedtotheminimum requiredthicknesscalculatedby
A-27 or A-32.
(b) Thecorrosionallowanceshallbespecifiedinthedesigndatasheetandshallnotbetakenas
less than zero.
(c) Whenback-calculatingtheMaximumAllowableWorkingPressure(MAWP),theeffectivecor-
roded thickness equals the nominal thickness minus the corrosion allowance.
A-27 Thickness of Shells Under Internal Pressure
(a) The minimum required thickness of shells subjected to internal pressure shall not be less
than that computed by the following formulas, per A-27(c) and A-27(d).
(b) TheallowablestressSshallbeobtainedperA-23,andthejointefficiencyEshallbeobtained
from Table B-12 (see B-12).
(c) Cylindrical Shells.
A-27(c)(1) â€” Inside Radius Formula:
t = (P Ã— R) / (S Ã— E âˆ’ 0.6 Ã— P)
where:
â€¢ t = minimum required thickness, mm
â€¢ P = design pressure, MPa
â€¢ R = inside radius of the shell course, mm
â€¢ S = maximum allowable stress at design temperature, MPa (see Table M-1)
1

â€¢ E = joint efficiency (see Table B-12)
Thisformulaapplieswhen: tâ‰¤R/2ANDPâ‰¤0.385Ã—SÃ—E.Whentheseconditionsarenotsatisfied,
the rules of A-27(c)(3) shall be applied (not covered here).
A-27(c)(2) â€” Outside Radius Formula:
t = (P Ã— Ro) / (S Ã— E + 0.4 Ã— P)
where Ro is the outside radius of the shell course. Applicable when P â‰¤ 0.385 Ã— S Ã— E.
(d) Spherical Shells.
A-27(d) â€” Spherical Shell Formula:
t = (P Ã— R) / (2 Ã— S Ã— E âˆ’ 0.2 Ã— P)
Applicable when t â‰¤ 0.356 Ã— R AND P â‰¤ 0.665 Ã— S Ã— E.
A-32 Formed Heads
Formed heads (ellipsoidal, torispherical, hemispherical, conical) are covered by this paragraph.
For a 2:1 ellipsoidal head:
t = (P Ã— D) / (2 Ã— S Ã— E âˆ’ 0.2 Ã— P)
where D is the inside diameter. The joint efficiency E shall be taken from Table B-12 and the
allowable stress S from Table M-1 per A-23.
Part B â€” Welding Requirements
B-12 Joint Efficiency
(a) The joint efficiency E used in the thickness formulas of A-27 and A-32 shall be taken from
Table B-12 as a function of the joint type (Types 1 through 6) and the extent of radiographic
examination (Full RT, Spot RT, or None).
(b) Table B-12 â€” Joint Efficiency Values:
Joint Type Description Full RT Spot RT No RT
1 Double-welded butt, full penetration 1.00 0.85 0.70
2 Single-welded butt with backing strip 0.90 0.80 0.65
3 Single-welded butt without backing 0.80 0.70 0.60
4 Double full-fillet lap 0.75 0.65 0.55
5 Single full-fillet lap with plug welds 0.70 0.60 0.50
6 Single full-fillet lap without plug welds 0.65 0.55 0.45
(c) The radiography level selected shall be consistent with the service conditions and shall be
documented.
2

M-23 Material Restrictions
(a) SM-516 Gr 70 shall not be used at design temperatures exceeding 500Â°C.
(b) SM-106GrBshallnotbeusedatdesigntemperaturesexceeding500Â°Candisnotpermitted
for cyclic service above 400Â°C without additional analysis.
Appendix â€” Worked Example (Informative)
For a cylindrical shell with P = 1.5 MPa, inside diameter 1800 mm, design temperature 350Â°C,
material SM-516 Gr 70, Type 1 joint with full RT, and 3.0 mm corrosion allowance, the minimum
requiredthicknessperA-27(c)(1)iscomputedusingS=114MPa(TableM-1)andE=1.00(TableB-
12). Afteraddingcorrosionallowanceandroundingtothenextstandardplate,thedesignthickness
is selected.
